steps.title: Spot the elaborative clause
steps.content: On the left side of the blank is an independent clause: divergent strategies [verb]. On the right is a whereas clause that elaborates by showing how the divergent strategies split geographically—one becoming dominant in Europe, the other among nomadic herders.

steps.title: Apply the colon rule
steps.content:
Structure: "divergent strategies for preserving milk [blank] whereas rennet-coagulated...became key...acid-heat...became common..." The blank follows the subject + verb of the main clause; what follows is an elaboration showing how those divergent strategies split into two regional variants.

Rule: a colon after an independent clause introduces elaboration or explanation.

A. emerged and — "and" is a coordinating conjunction that joins equals. But "whereas rennet..." is not a parallel clause being added; it's an elaboration. Adding "and" creates a grammatical mismatch. Eliminated.

B. emerged — no punctuation leaves the "whereas" clause attached directly to the main clause with no boundary marker. A "whereas" clause elaborating on how divergence played out needs a separator. Eliminated.

C. emerged, — a comma cannot introduce an elaborating explanation after a complete independent clause; that role belongs to a colon. Eliminated.

D. emerged: — the colon correctly signals that what follows explains how the divergent strategies manifested. Correct.

D is correct.
